parsed.production_notes: The video features a real human actor in a natural kitchen setting. The lighting, movements, and interactions with physical objects (washing dishes) are realistic and consistent with human recording.

characters.appearance: Young woman in her 20s, slim build, fair complexion. She has blonde hair tied up in a messy bun with some loose strands framing her face. She has dark eyeliner and defined eyebrows.
characters.wardrobe: Wearing a loose, oversized brown t-shirt with a graphic print on the front.
characters.demeanor: Casual, slightly frustrated initially, but becomes engaged and happy as she learns.

characters.appearance: Digital avatar of a young woman with reddish-brown hair, visible only on the tablet screen.
characters.wardrobe: Not clearly visible, appears to be wearing a light-colored top.

scenes.setting: A modern kitchen with white cabinets, a dark speckled countertop, and a stainless steel sink. A tablet is propped up on the counter next to the sink.
scenes.camera: Medium shot, slightly low angle, capturing the woman at the sink and the tablet on the counter.

panels.panel_visual_description: A young woman with blonde hair in a messy bun is standing at a kitchen sink, washing a stainless steel pot with a sponge. She is wearing an oversized brown t-shirt. To her right, on the dark speckled countertop, a tablet is propped up, displaying a digital avatar of a woman with reddish hair. The kitchen has white cabinets and a blue light strip under the upper cabinets. The woman looks between the pot she is washing and the tablet screen, engaging in a conversation.

scenes.visual_description: The scene shows a woman washing dishes while interacting with a language learning app on her tablet. Text overlays in Spanish and English appear on screen, translating the conversation. A banner with Spanish flags hangs at the top of the frame. The woman makes mistakes, is corrected by the app, and then repeats the correct phrases, showing a progression from frustration to satisfaction.
scenes.audio_transcript:
Character A says: Odio el español.
[I hate Spanish.]
Character B says: ¿Cómo te llamas?
[What's your name?]
Character A says: Me llamo Nesly.
[My name is Nesly.]
Character B says: ¿Cómo estás?
[How are you?]
Character A says: Estoy bien, gracias.
[I am fine, thank you.]
Character B says: ¿Cuántos años tienes?
[How old are you?]
Character A says: Soy 24.
[I am 24.]
Character B says: No digas "Soy 24."
[Don't say "Soy 24."]
Character B says: Di "Tengo 24 años."
[Say "Tengo 24 años."]
Character A says: Ah, tengo 24 años.
[Ah, I am 24 years old.]
Character B says: ¿Por qué odias el español?
[Why do you hate Spanish?]
Character A says: Estoy sin trabajo por mi mal español.
[I am jobless because of my bad Spanish.]
Character B says: No digas "sin trabajo."
[Don't say "sin trabajo."]
Character B says: Di "desempleado."
[Say "desempleado."]
Character A says: Ah!
[Ah!]
Character A says: Estoy desempleado por mi mal español.
[I am unemployed because of my bad Spanish.]
Character B says: ¡Eso sonó 99% con acento nativo, muy bien!
[That was 99% in a native accent, great job!]
Character A says: ¿Puedes enseñarme español así todos los días?
[Can you teach me Spanish like this every day?]
Character B says: Solo descarga Praktika.
[Just download Praktika.]
Character B says: Corregiré tus errores de pronunciación y practicaré contigo todos los días.
[I'll fix your pronunciation mistakes and practice with you every day.]
Character A says: Vale, voy a descargar Praktika ahora mismo.
[Okay, I'll download Praktika right now.]

Odio el español.
(I hate Spanish.)
¿Cómo te llamas?
(What's your name?)
Me llamo Nesly.
(My name is Nesly.)
¿Cómo estás?
(How are you?)
Estoy bien, gracias.
(I am fine, thank you.)

(How old are you?)
Soy 24.
(I am 24.)
No digas "Soy 24."
(Don't say "Soy 24.")
Di "Tengo 24 años."
(Say "Tengo 24 años.")
Tengo 24 años.
(I am 24 years old.)
¿Por qué odias el español?
(Why do you hate Spanish?)
Estoy sin trabajo
(I am jobless)
por mi mal español.
(because of my bad Spanish.)
No digas "sin trabajo."
(Don't say "sin trabajo.")
Di "desempleado."
(Say "desempleado.")
Ah!
Estoy desempleado
(I am unemployed)
por mi mal español.
(because of my bad Spanish.)
¡Eso sonó 99%
(That was 99%)
con acento nativo,
(in a native accent,)
muy bien!
(great job!)
¿Puedes enseñarme
(Can you teach me)
español así todos los días?

Solo descarga Praktika.

scenes.visual_description: The scene is a static end card with a light purple background. The Praktika app logo is centered, with 'Download on the App Store' and 'GET IT ON Google Play' buttons below it.
scenes.on_screen_text:
Praktika
Download on the App Store
GET IT ON Google Play
